items.headline: Schroder’s halftime speech, strong fourth quarter spark Cavaliers over Raptors
items.blurb: Schroder’s halftime speech, strong fourth quarter spark Cavaliers over Raptors — Schroder scored 11 points in the fourth quarter as the Cavaliers came back to beat the Toronto Raptors 125-120 in Game 5 of their first-round series.

items.blurb: ‘Our back is against the wall’: Rajakovic on Raptors’ attrition — Toronto Raptors head coach Darko Rajakovic talks about the fluctuating health and attrition issues among his players after Game 5, and what it will take for the Raptors to stay alive in Game 6.

items.headline: Cooper Flagg looks ahead as he accepts Rookie of the Year Award
items.blurb: Cooper Flagg looks ahead as he accepts Rookie of the Year Award — Cooper Flagg said he’s typically gotten some advance notice when he’s won major awards in the past, but he had no idea what the results were going to be as he watched the Rookie of the Year announcement on Monday.

items.headline: NBA Game 5 Highlights: Rockets 99, Lakers 93
items.blurb: NBA Game 5 Highlights: Rockets 99, Lakers 93 — Jabari Smith Jr. scored 22 points, Alperen Sengun added 14 along with nine rebounds and eight assists, and the Rockets staved off elimination with a 99-93 Game 6 win over the Lakers.

items.headline: Rockets avoid elimination again, beat Lakers in Game 5
items.blurb: Rockets avoid elimination again, beat Lakers in Game 5 — Jabari Smith Jr. scored 22 points, Tari Eason added 18 and the Houston Rockets avoided playoff elimination for the second straight game with a 99-93 victory over the Lakers in Game 5 on Wednesday night, trimming Los Angeles’ lead in the first-round series to 3-2.

items.headline: Lakers get major Luka Doncic injury update
items.blurb: Lakers get major Luka Doncic injury update — The Los Angeles Lakers have gotten by just fine without their two leading scorers.

items.headline: NBA legend Magic Johnson issues apology to the 76ers
items.blurb: NBA legend Magic Johnson issues apology to the 76ers — Magic Johnson counted out the Philadelphia 76ers too soon.

items.headline: Reed Sheppard's clutch play late helps Rockets stave off elimination vs. Lakers on LeBron James' historic night
items.blurb: Reed Sheppard's clutch play late helps Rockets stave off elimination vs. Lakers on LeBron James' historic night — A historic night from LeBron James and the long-awaited return of Austin Reaves were still not enough to prevent the short-handed Houston Rockets from taking Wednesday's Game 5.

items.headline: The 'Active 20-PPG NBA seasons' quiz
items.blurb: The 'Active 20-PPG NBA seasons' quiz — How many of the active NBA players who have averaged 20 points-per-game or more in at least three seasons can you name in six minutes?

items.headline: Raptors face tall order in do-or-die Game 6 as key players battle injuries
items.blurb: Raptors face tall order in do-or-die Game 6 as key players battle injuries — Michael Grange joins Tim Micallef and Faizal Khamisa to discuss Brandon Ingram’s potential availability for Game 6, Scottie Barnes playing at less than 100 percent, why the Raptors missed Ingram’s scoring mentality late in Game 5, and more.

items.headline: NBA Game 5 Highlights: Cavaliers 125, Raptors 120
items.blurb: NBA Game 5 Highlights: Cavaliers 125, Raptors 120 — James Harden and Evan Mobley each had 24 points, Dennis Schroder had 19 points in 21 minutes off the bench, and the Cleveland Cavaliers took Game 5 with a 125-120 win over the Toronto Raptors to take a 3-2 series lead.

items.headline: Raptors running out of rope after Ingram injury, Game 5 loss
items.blurb: Raptors running out of rope after Ingram injury, Game 5 loss — For much of the series, the Raptors have done the unthinkable — been the better team. But with injuries and a Game 5 loss hanging over their head, they’re starting to run out of rope. Michael Grange has the story from Cleveland.

items.headline: Cavaliers ride hot hand of Dennis Schroder to take pivotal Game 5 against Raptors
items.blurb: Cavaliers ride hot hand of Dennis Schroder to take pivotal Game 5 against Raptors — Even a poor defensive performance in the first half could not keep the Cleveland Cavaliers from making it five wins in as many games for the home team in the first-round series against the Toronto Raptors with Wednesday's 125-120 comeback win.

items.headline: NBA Game 5 Highlights: Pistons 116, Magic 109
items.blurb: NBA Game 5 Highlights: Pistons 116, Magic 109 — Cade Cunningham carried the offensive load with 45 points on 23 shots, Tobias Harris added 23 points with eight rebounds, and the Detroit Pistons secured a 116-109 win in Game 5 over the Orlando Magic.

items.headline: Cunningham stars as Pistons beat Magic in Game 5 to stave off elimination
items.blurb: Cunningham stars as Pistons beat Magic in Game 5 to stave off elimination — Cade Cunningham scored a franchise playoff record 45 points, including a step-back jumper with 32 seconds left, and the top-seeded Detroit Pistons beat the eighth-seeded Orlando Magic 116-109 on Wednesday night in Game 5 of their first-round series to stave off elimination.

items.headline: Brandon Ingram Exits Game 5 Loss With Right Heel Inflammation
items.blurb: Brandon Ingram Exits Game 5 Loss With Right Heel Inflammation — Brandon Ingram exited Game 5 after 11 minutes due to right heel inflammation. Ingram left the game early in the second quarter as the Toronto Raptors went on to lose by a 125-120 margin. The Raptors were already without Immanuel Quickley due to a hamstring injury. Ingram is averaging just 14.8 points coming into Game 5 at the Clevelan

items.headline: Pistons avoid elimination vs. Magic behind Cade Cunningham's historic performance
items.blurb: Pistons avoid elimination vs. Magic behind Cade Cunningham's historic performance — As the Orlando Magic stormed back in the final six minutes, the Detroit Pistons couldn't get baskets from anyone but Cade Cunningham. So their All-Star guard did it himself.

items.headline: Victor Wembanyama responds to those criticizing him for 'crying'
items.blurb: Victor Wembanyama responds to those criticizing him for 'crying' — Victor Wembanyama is not afraid to wear his emotions on his sleeve.

items.headline: Raptors’ Brandon Ingram exits Game 5 vs. Cavaliers with heel inflammation
items.blurb: Raptors’ Brandon Ingram exits Game 5 vs. Cavaliers with heel inflammation — Brandon Ingram exited Wednesday’s Game 5 showdown after dealing with right heel inflammation, the team announced before halftime.

items.blurb: Cavaliers’ Mitchell splashes logo three to end first vs. Raptors — Watch as Cavaliers guard Donovan Mitchell sinks a buzzer-beater three-pointer from just beyond half-court to end the first quarter strong vs. the Raptors.

items.headline: Scottie Barnes sets the tone with two early blocks in Game 5
items.blurb: Scottie Barnes sets the tone with two early blocks in Game 5 — Watch as Scottie Barnes sets the tone on defence by blocking James Harden’s three-pointer and Evan Mobley’s floater in quick succession early in Game 5 against the Cavaliers.

items.headline: Skip Bayless calls LeBron James' GOAT case 'laughable'
items.blurb: Skip Bayless calls LeBron James' GOAT case 'laughable' — In an interview with ESPN's Dave McMenamin, Los Angeles Lakers star LeBron James was asked the age-old GOAT debate question. While James was very respectful about Chicago Bulls legend Michael Jordan, he did not hide that he feels he is a “one of one.”

items.headline: Magic’s Franz Wagner out for Game 5 vs. Pistons with calf injury
items.blurb: Magic’s Franz Wagner out for Game 5 vs. Pistons with calf injury — Orlando Magic forward Franz Wagner is out of the lineup with a strained right calf against the Detroit Pistons in Game 5 of their first-round playoff series on Wednesday night.

items.blurb: Is AJ Dybantsa the Fernando Mendoza of the NBA? — Heading into the 2026 NFL draft, there was no question that superstar quarterback Fernando Mendoza of the Indiana Hoosiers was going to the Las Vegas Raiders. Will the NBA Draft follow a similar pattern with Dybantsa?

items.headline: Another Victor Wembanyama block party sends Trail Blazers home
items.blurb: Another Victor Wembanyama block party sends Trail Blazers home — Even when Victor Wembanyama was out with concussion symptoms, the Portland Trail Blazers struggled to score against the San Antonio Spurs. With Wemby back in the lineup, the Blazers offense was totally shut down.

items.headline: Is it time for star Devin Booker, Phoenix Suns to split?
items.blurb: Is it time for star Devin Booker, Phoenix Suns to split? — The Phoenix Suns and Devin Booker saw their 2026 postseason end early, falling to the reigning NBA champion Oklahoma City Thunder in the first round. The Suns exceeded expectations, but the franchise has struggled to replicate the success of an NBA Finals appearance in 2021.

items.blurb: DeAndre Jordan Wins 2025-26 Twyman-Stokes Teammate Of The Year Award — DeAndre Jordan has won the 2025-26 Twyman-Stokes Teammate of the Year , beating out Jrue Holiday, Jeff Green and Garrett Temple. Holiday has won the award three times in his career. Jordan signed with the New Orleans Pelicans this season after spending the previous three with the Denver Nuggets. items.headline: Every NBA Champion Since 2015 Has Won First Round In Five Or Fewer Games
items.blurb: Every NBA Champion Since 2015 Has Won First Round In Five Or Fewer Games — The Oklahoma City Thunder swept the Phoenix Suns in the first round of the 2026 NBA Playoffs as they look to extend a remarkable 11-year pattern in which every NBA champion has advanced past the first round in five or fewer games. items.headline: AJ Dybantsa Converts Nike NIL Deal Into Pro Contract
items.blurb: AJ Dybantsa Converts Nike NIL Deal Into Pro Contract — BYU freshman AJ Dybantsa is converting his existing Nike NIL agreement into a professional endorsement contract as he prepares for the 2026 NBA Draft, he confirmed to ESPN. The 6-foot-9 forward is widely projected as a top pick in June . "It means everything," Dybantsa said. "They've been with me since Day 1. It's been a great partnership and

items.headline: Alperen Sengun: Full stat line in Game 5 win
items.blurb: Alperen Sengun: Full stat line in Game 5 win — Sengun supplied 14 points (5-9 FG, 4-4 FT), nine rebounds, eight assists, two steals and one block across 43 minutes in Wednesday's 99-93 win over the Lakers in Game 5 of the first round of the Western Conference playoffs. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.

items.headline: NBA Remains Focused On Starting European League For 2027-28 Season
items.blurb: NBA Remains Focused On Starting European League For 2027-28 Season — The NBA is continuing to target the 2027-28 season for the launch of NBA Europe. The NBA is now hoping to work with EuroLeague as partners, as opposed to being competitors. The NBA's European expansion is being led by deputy commissioner Mark Tatum. items.blurb: Pistons Interested In Bringing Isaac Bonga Back To NBA — The Detroit Pistons are looking towards next season, while still competing in the 2026 NBA Playoffs. The Pistons reportedly are close to agreeing with veteran forward Isaac Bonga on a deal that would bring him back to the NBA. Bonga is currently playing for Partizan in Serbia.
